LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: If the smart card is removed the LPFR must stop issuing invoices
ExpectedError: Service must not fiscalize new invoice
Notes: Seeded from L-PFR manual testing document

LayerNotes: Transport-normalization note aligned with REC-ERR-001 and kept outside the primary canonical contract layer
RuleText: Status and error codes may be returned as plain text while some ESIR implementations expect JSON
ExpectedError: Mismatch must be handled or normalized
Notes: Important reconciliation item

LayerNotes: Manual-test validation-priority behavior kept as an approval-facing integration rule
RuleText: ESIR expects errors sorted by priority so the user can remove them successfully
ExpectedError: Prioritized error list
Notes: Important for client-side error model

LayerNotes: The technical instruction and approval manuals strongly support this as an approval-sensitive design constraint
RuleText: Changes that alter functionality or receipt appearance require a new technical review and approval
ExpectedError: Reapproval workflow
Notes: Meta rule but important for versioning and traceability

LayerNotes: Canonical naming reconciliation rule retained for terminology stability
RuleText: The source materials changed buyer identification naming from PIB kupca to ID kupca
ExpectedError: Canonical buyer identification model
Notes: Important naming reconciliation rule

LayerNotes: Legal classification is normative while the font-emphasis detail is sharpened by technical and manual-test material
RuleText: Only promet and avans are fiscal receipts while copy training and proforma are fiscal documents
ExpectedError: Documents must be distinguished from fiscal receipts
Notes: Foundational Serbian legal distinction

LayerNotes: Legal classification is normative while the font-emphasis detail is sharpened by technical and manual-test material
RuleText: Copy training and proforma documents must visibly contain KOPIJA OBUKA or PREDRACUN and OVO NIJE FISKALNI RACUN
ExpectedError: Document must be visually distinguished
Notes: Required font size rule still needs extraction detail

LayerNotes: Normative refund-or-cancellation semantic retained as core domain meaning
RuleText: Serbian refund transaction bucket includes refund and cancellation or void
ExpectedError: Canonical mapping to Refund path
Notes: TaxCoreTransactionType Refund covers both

LayerNotes: Rulebook and FAQ material consistently support the payment-label set and mixed-payment allocation requirement
RuleText: A fiscal receipt may show more than one payment method and must show allocated amounts per method
ExpectedError: Allocated payment amounts must be present

LayerNotes: Rulebook and FAQ material consistently support the payment-label set and mixed-payment allocation requirement
RuleText: For on site food and drink service and bakery flows only a restricted payment option set may be offered
ExpectedError: Card check and instant may collapse under cash display option
Notes: Important Serbia specific UI rule

LayerNotes: Normative and technical sources align that QR and hyperlink are two renderings of the same verification path
RuleText: Printed fiscal receipt must contain QR code while electronic fiscal receipt must contain verification hyperlink
ExpectedError: Verification data must be present in the correct form

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: referentDocumentDT may be used only when referentDocumentNumber is also present
ExpectedError: Invalid reference combination should fail

LayerNotes: Official Accept-Language journal-selection rule retained as protocol truth
RuleText: Accept-Language header selects invoice journal language and unsupported languages return HTTP 406
ExpectedError: 406 if none of the requested languages are supported

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: VerifyPin request body must contain exactly four ASCII digits
ExpectedError: 2806 or 2100 or 2110 depending failure mode

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: If VerifyPin returns 0100 E-SDC remembers the PIN in volatile memory until card removal or device shutdown
ExpectedError: PIN need not be sent before every operation

LayerNotes: Operational configuration advice retained as guidance rather than hard protocol shape
RuleText: Environment URLs and names should not be hardcoded but configurable or extracted from certificate
ExpectedError: Use environment parameters or certificate metadata

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: referentDocumentNumber is mandatory for copies and refunds and some normal sale or advance scenarios
ExpectedError: Invalid reference combination should fail

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: In Serbian buyer identification flows buyerId must be entered using codebook prefix and payload in the form code:value
ExpectedError: Invalid or unknown buyer identification value

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: In Serbian buyer identification flows buyerCostCenterId uses codebook prefix and payload in the form code:value
ExpectedError: Invalid or unknown optional buyer field value

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: When buyer identification is used the receipt must display ID kupca and may display Opciono polje kupca if needed
ExpectedError: Rendered receipt must contain buyer identification fields

LayerNotes: Baseline corporate-card sale rule retained after REC-RUL-011 split
RuleText: For corporate card retail sales the system records buyer identification and optional field 50 for card number and payment method is always Voucher
ExpectedError: Receipt must be sale with voucher payment and buyer optional field 50

LayerNotes: Baseline corporate-card aggregate refund rule retained after REC-RUL-011 split
RuleText: For aggregate refund of previously invoiced corporate card sales optional field 60 must carry a calendar-month date range and referentDocumentNumber plus referentDocumentDT must point to the last invoice in that period
ExpectedError: Receipt must be refund with field 60 and valid reference

LayerNotes: Baseline refund-buyer-identification rule retained after REC-RUL-011 split
RuleText: For documented refund flows that return money directly to the buyer and are not the special noncash refund guidance branch the baseline is to enter buyer identification and a reference to the original sale or advance invoice
ExpectedError: Refund receipt is invalid without buyer identification and reference in the documented baseline scenario
Notes: Second-pass baseline rule; keep the lower-authority noncash refund nuance separate in RUL-091 and RUL-094

LayerNotes: Baseline immediate-correction buyer-ID rule retained after REC-RUL-011 split
RuleText: If an invoice with buyer identification is not cancelled immediately after the transaction a new invoice with buyer identification must not be generated as a correction
ExpectedError: Subsequent invoice would be considered invalid
Notes: Extracted from explanatory text before section 9.4

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: Secure element APDU commands require selecting the SE applet first
ExpectedError: Subsequent commands are routed to SE applet only after Select SE Applet

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: From secure element version 3.2.5 some commands support optional CRC and change P1 P2 accordingly
TriggerContext: SignInvoice and GetLastSignedInvoice and forwarded directive flows
ExpectedError: Command framing must match SE version

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: From secure element version 3.2.8 invoice timestamp used for Sign Invoice must be within certificate NotBefore and NotAfter
ExpectedError: SE returns 0x6308 when timestamp is outside certificate validity

LayerNotes: Version-gated SE command availability is part of the canonical APDU integration model
RuleText: Availability and exact content of secure element commands depends on applet version
ExpectedError: Capability detection should rely on Get Secure Element Version and command version gates

LayerNotes: Official mapping from Verify PIN APDU results to general status codes is core integration truth
RuleText: Secure element Verify PIN status words map to general E-SDC codes 2100 and 2110
ExpectedError: POS must receive mapped general status code not raw APDU word

LayerNotes: Current SignInvoice byte layout is part of the canonical byte-level contract
RuleText: Current SignInvoice APDU layout uses 7-byte amount fields and places tax category count at byte 57
ExpectedError: Payload offsets must follow current spec

LayerNotes: Legacy sample mismatch remains a reconciliation note rather than current protocol truth
RuleText: Older SESignInvoice sample uses 8-byte amount fields and therefore shifted offsets while V3 sample and current documentation use the 7-byte layout
ExpectedError: Canonical catalog follows current documentation and V3 sample

LayerNotes: Shared LastSignedInvoice response layout is part of the canonical APDU response model
RuleText: Get Last Signed Invoice returns the same data structure as Sign Invoice response
ExpectedError: Parser for LastSignedInvoiceResponse should reuse SignInvoiceApduResponse layout

LayerNotes: The crosswalk is useful and well supported but it is still a reconciliation layer between Serbian display labels and the generic protocol enum
RuleText: For Serbian integrations the strongly supported crosswalk is Gotovina to Cash Platna kartica to Card Ček to Check Prenos na račun to WireTransfer Vaučer to Voucher Drugo bezgotovinsko plaćanje to Other and Instant plaćanje to MobileMoney
TriggerContext: Mapping legal display values to protocol enum values
ExpectedError: Keep legal and protocol enums separate but use this crosswalk when serializing PaymentItem.paymentType
Notes: Second-pass derived serialization rule and not a canonical enum source; inferred from official generic PaymentType lists plus the Serbian localization of the reference app

LayerNotes: PKI applet AID version gate is part of the canonical smart-card integration model
RuleText: SelectPKIApplet uses AID A000000397425446590201 for smart card secure element versions below 3.1.1 and A000000397425446590009 for versions 3.1.1 and above
ExpectedError: Wrong AID selection will fail PKI command routing
Notes: Reference implementation currently uses the legacy 0201 AID only

LayerNotes: Shared SE and PKI PIN rule is official protocol behavior on relevant versions
RuleText: From secure element version 3.2.2 the same PIN value is used for both the secure element applet and the PKI applet
ExpectedError: Do not model SE PIN and PKI PIN as unrelated secrets on those versions
Notes: PKI certificate access over PKCS11 depends on the same PIN object

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification code 12 is the documented composite form 12:PIB:JBKJS while the other documented buyerId values follow the simpler code:value shape
ExpectedError: Invalid structure should fail buyer identification validation
Notes: This is the only buyerId codebook entry that explicitly adds a second payload separator

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: Serbian buyer optional field code 60 must use the exact syntax 60:ddmmgggg_ddmmgggg and represents a calendar-month refund period covering 00:00:00 of the first date through 23:59:59 of the last date
ExpectedError: Invalid code 60 structure or non-month period should fail validation
Notes: The technical guide also explains the full-day coverage semantics of the entered range

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: If an invoice pushes the secure element over the no-proof-of-audit limit while internet is available the invoice may still be signed but LPFR must show a visible audit warning and block the next invoice until Proof of Audit is received
ExpectedError: Second invoice blocked until proof arrives and issuance resumes afterwards
Notes: Manual testing expects the proof from SUF after roughly five minutes

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: If an invoice pushes the secure element over the no-proof-of-audit limit while internet is unavailable the first invoice may still be signed but the next invoice must be blocked until the local-audit proof workflow completes
ExpectedError: Second invoice blocked until proof can be applied
Notes: Manual testing continues with the local audit export and proof application workflow

LayerNotes: Manual-tested local-audit concurrency behavior retained as operational guidance
RuleText: LPFR must remain able to issue invoices while local audit is running
ExpectedError: Invoice issuance continues during audit export
Notes: Manual scenario explicitly starts local audit and successfully issues another invoice

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: Removing and reinserting the smart card invalidates the previously cached PIN and invoice issuance must remain blocked until a valid PIN is entered again
ExpectedError: Issuance blocked until VerifyPin succeeds again
Notes: Extends the PIN caching rule with reinsertion behavior

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: Get Status may still return HTTP 200 while exposing multiple general status codes for degraded states such as no card or PIN required
ExpectedError: Clients must inspect the gsc array instead of relying only on HTTP status
Notes: Manual scenarios show combinations like 1300 plus 1500 plus 0210 and 1500 plus 0210

LayerNotes: This family is driven mainly by manual-test expectations rather than protocol text
RuleText: ESIR must refuse invoice issuance when it cannot communicate with LPFR or VPFR or when the required security element or client certificate is unavailable
TriggerContext: ESIR issuance without a working fiscalization path
ExpectedError: Receipt must not be issued and a user-facing notification must be shown
Notes: Manual testing disconnects LPFR removes the BE or disables the VPFR certificate

LayerNotes: This family is driven mainly by manual-test expectations rather than protocol text
RuleText: ESIR must not omit or alter any mandatory fiscal data returned by the PFR and displayed receipt data must match both the Administration Portal and the electronic journal
ExpectedError: Rendered receipt must faithfully reflect the fiscalized data
Notes: Covers printed and electronic delivery outputs

LayerNotes: Portal and receipt display expectation retained as approval-facing rendering guidance
RuleText: GTIN entry must be supported when present on an item and although GTIN need not be printed on the receipt the unit of measure must appear as part of the item name and GTIN must remain visible on the Administration Portal
ExpectedError: Receipt and portal views must stay consistent

LayerNotes: Receipt-frame containment retained as approval-facing rendering guidance
RuleText: All fiscal receipt elements must appear within the fiscal frame lines while only commercial or non-fiscal material may be rendered outside them
ExpectedError: Fiscal data must stay inside the fiscal frame

LayerNotes: Legal classification is normative while the font-emphasis detail is sharpened by technical and manual-test material
RuleText: Copy Training and Proforma receipts must show the message OVO NIJE FISKALNI RACUN in letters twice the size of the remaining receipt text
ExpectedError: Required notice must be visually dominant
Notes: The ESIR manual makes the double-size font an explicit review point

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: ESIR time must be omitted for all receipt and transaction types except Advance Sale that records a wire transfer whose banking evidence is older than the receipt day
TriggerContext: Receipt rendering and advanced advance-sale scenarios
ExpectedError: ESIR time appears only in the documented exception flow
Notes: Approval samples require an Advance Sale where ESIR time is at least one day ahead of PFR time

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
AppliesTo: Reference time on copy and refund
RuleText: All Copy receipts and all Refund transactions must contain both reference number and reference time while Sale and Advance receipts may include them only when the use case requires it
TriggerContext: ESIR issuance of copy or refund receipts
ExpectedError: Receipt is invalid if required reference fields are missing
Notes: Manual note warns that missing reference time can cause tax-rate mismatches for older referenced receipts

LayerNotes: This family is driven mainly by manual-test expectations rather than protocol text
RuleText: ESIR must authenticate with the PFR each time it starts using the relevant BE credential or smart-card secret
ExpectedError: Startup is not complete until the PFR connection is established
Notes: Manual scenario covers both LPFR and VPFR cases

LayerNotes: This family is driven mainly by manual-test expectations rather than protocol text
RuleText: If ESIR supports electronic delivery it must be able to deliver every supported receipt type through the selected electronic channel without dropping mandatory fiscal data
ExpectedError: Delivered receipt preserves all mandatory content

LayerNotes: This family is driven mainly by manual-test expectations rather than protocol text
RuleText: ESIR must display item prices and totals rounded to at least two decimals using half-up rounding
ExpectedError: Displayed values must follow the documented rounding examples
Notes: Examples include 10.267 to 10.27 20.143 to 20.14 and 1.2177 to 1.22

LayerNotes: This family is driven mainly by manual-test expectations rather than protocol text
RuleText: ESIR must use only active tax rates exposed by the PFR and manual entry of a non-existent tax rate must block invoice issuance
TriggerContext: Manual tax selection or stale cached tax data
ExpectedError: Receipt must not be issued with unknown tax rates

LayerNotes: Copy-refund signature-line expectation retained as approval-facing rendering guidance
RuleText: Copy Refund receipts must include a customer signature line
ExpectedError: Missing signature line is a manual-test failure
Notes: Derived from the common-mistakes section

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: Copy Refund receipts must reference the original Promet Refund receipt and not a Copy Sale receipt
ExpectedError: Wrong referenced document chain is invalid
Notes: Derived from the common-mistakes section

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
AppliesTo: Optional field omission versus empty string
RuleText: For optional JSON request fields omitting the property is not the same as sending an empty string because empty string values trigger validation failures such as 2802 or 2803
ExpectedError: Absent optional values should be omitted rather than serialized as empty strings
Notes: The specification explicitly uses buyerId as the example

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
AppliesTo: Buyer identification generic protocol length limits
RuleText: At the protocol layer buyerId allows at most 20 Unicode characters and buyerCostCenterId allows at most 50 Unicode characters before any Serbian codebook-specific validation is applied
TriggerContext: Invoice with buyer identification or optional buyer field
ExpectedError: Overlong values should fail length validation before deeper semantic checks
Notes: Jurisdiction-specific semantics are layered on top of these generic limits

LayerNotes: Open-ended environment-endpoints schema is part of the canonical bootstrap contract
RuleText: Get Environment Parameters returns a required endpoints object whose known keys taxpayerAdminPortal taxCoreApi vsdc and root are repeated across official examples but clients must tolerate additional keys because the response field list can grow
ExpectedError: Use the known keys when present but keep parsers forward-compatible
Notes: Repeated official examples and the reference app make the four keys strong known members rather than one-off helper data

LayerNotes: Structured validation-error payload contract is part of the canonical API surface
RuleText: Structured validation failures use a top-level message and a modelState array where each entry carries a property path and one or more shared status or error codes
TriggerContext: HTTP validation failures from JSON endpoints
ExpectedError: Clients can surface field-level errors without parsing free-form text
Notes: ModelErrors and ModelError are explicitly defined in the specification

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification codes 10 11 13 14 15 16 20 21 22 23 30 31 32 33 34 35 36 and 40 use the generic code:value syntax while only code 12 extends it to code:PIB:JBKJS
ExpectedError: Unexpected extra separators should fail parsing before document-specific validation
Notes: This narrows the remaining open work to per-document payload character classes and lengths

LayerNotes: The codebook and technical instruction provide direct support for the code-prefixed syntax families and rendering baseline
RuleText: Serbian buyer optional field codes 20 21 30 31 32 33 and 50 use the generic code:value syntax while code 60 uniquely requires a date-range payload
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian optional buyer field
ExpectedError: Unexpected date-range or separator structure should fail parsing before document-specific validation
Notes: This narrows the remaining open work to per-document payload character classes and lengths

LayerNotes: Buyer optional-field generic payload class is a useful validator derivation rather than an explicit published schema
AppliesTo: Buyer optional field generic payload class
RuleText: The Serbian buyerCostCenterId payload is documented as an alphanumeric designation of the related document or purpose unless a stricter format is documented for a specific code such as 60
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian optional buyer field
ExpectedError: Validators should accept general alphanumeric document markers and then apply code-specific tightening where available
Notes: This supports SNPDV LNPDV PPO-PDV and corporate-card number scenarios without overfitting them to digits only

LayerNotes: Number-family split remains a derivation layer because exact national regexes are not published
RuleText: Within the Serbian codebook the payloads behind codes 10 11 12 14 15 and 16 are explicitly described as named number systems PIB JMBG JBKJS and BPG and should therefore be treated as number-family identifiers even though their exact national lengths are not specified here
ExpectedError: Use numeric-family parsing for these codes and keep exact length checks as a later stricter layer
Notes: This is stronger than pure free text but still intentionally stops short of asserting national regexes not stated in the source

LayerNotes: Foreign-TIN alphanumeric allowance is a derived validator bridge from generic TIN guidance to Serbian code 40
RuleText: Validators must not assume that a foreign TIN is numeric-only because TaxCore documentation explicitly states that TINs in some countries may contain letters
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian buyer identification code 40
ExpectedError: Buyer identification code 40 should allow alphanumeric payloads rather than a digits-only regex
Notes: This bridges the generic TIN formatting guidance to the Serbian foreign-TIN code

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
AppliesTo: Security element mandatory for fiscal status
RuleText: A receipt issued without using the security element cannot be treated as a fiscal receipt
ExpectedError: Issuance without the BE is legally non-fiscal
Notes: This is a direct legal rule not just an implementation detail

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: The security element is personalized to a specific taxpayer business premises and business room and must not be used by an unauthorized party in another premises room or mismatched fiscal device
ExpectedError: The BE must be bound to the intended EFU installation scope
Notes: This sharply limits cross-device or cross-location reuse

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: After each transaction the security element must maintain and increment counters for invoice sequence receipt-kind and transaction splits tax-rate totals and overall turnover and tax totals
ExpectedError: Counter integrity is a mandatory BE responsibility
Notes: The rulebook states both the counter families and that they increase after each transaction

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: The Serbian security element may be issued only in the documented forms smart card smart SD card USB token or protected file such as PFX
ExpectedError: Supported deployment options should map to one of the normative BE forms

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: The BE certificate validity window is at least one year and at most four years and after expiry the BE must no longer be used
ExpectedError: Expired BE blocks further valid fiscalization
Notes: The same rule also allows requesting a replacement within 30 days before expiry

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: A new security element may be requested within the 30-day window before expiry of the current one
ExpectedError: Renewal should be tracked ahead of certificate expiration

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: If the security element is stolen damaged or destroyed the taxpayer must notify the Tax Authority electronically within three days so it can be deactivated
ExpectedError: Deactivation notice must be sent through the Tax Authority portal within three days

LayerNotes: The BE rulebook provides the strongest baseline and taxpayer-facing guidance only adds operational detail
RuleText: The certificate inside the security element is used both to reproduce the taxpayer digital signature on fiscal receipts and to reproduce the protected taxpayer secret when connecting to the Tax Authority system
ExpectedError: Do not model BE certificates as signing-only artifacts

LayerNotes: These primitives are directly supported by concepts and protocol material
AppliesTo: UID canonical format and certificate binding
RuleText: UID is a secure-element identifier that always consists of 8 uppercase alphanumeric characters and is stored in the digital certificate Subject SERIALNUMBER field
TriggerContext: Certificate parsing invoice-number composition and status payloads
ExpectedError: Parsers and validators should treat UID as a strict 8-character uppercase token

LayerNotes: These primitives are directly supported by concepts and protocol material
RuleText: PFX certificate password is always an 8-character uppercase alphanumeric code
ExpectedError: Reject or flag malformed PFX password captures before attempting certificate installation

LayerNotes: These primitives are directly supported by concepts and protocol material
RuleText: PAC is always a 6-character uppercase alphanumeric code used to confirm authorization when issuing fiscal invoices with a PFX certificate
TriggerContext: Connected V-SDC authentication with PFX certificate
ExpectedError: Reject malformed PAC inputs before attempting authenticated requests

LayerNotes: Normative and technical sources align that QR and hyperlink are two renderings of the same verification path
RuleText: Printed QR code is a machine-readable representation of the verification URL and must respect the documented minimum size of 40x40mm while electronic receipts may use the clickable verification URL instead
ExpectedError: Printed receipts must render a scannable QR code and electronic receipts must preserve the verification hyperlink

LayerNotes: These primitives are directly supported by concepts and protocol material
AppliesTo: MRC canonical structure and audit obligation
RuleText: Manufacturer Registration Code must follow the three-part ProductCode-ProductVersionCode-DeviceSerialNumber structure where ProductCode has 2 alphanumeric characters ProductVersionCode has 4 alphanumeric characters and all three parts are mandatory for audit packages
TriggerContext: E-SDC identity metadata and audit package generation
ExpectedError: MRC serializers should preserve the three-part structure and never omit a part in audit data

LayerNotes: The JSON mode is explicit in the applications guidance but not part of the core LPFR protocol spec
RuleText: Public receipt-verification URL may return machine-readable JSON when called with Accept application/json in addition to the human-facing verification page
ExpectedError: Consumers can automate verification lookup without screen scraping

LayerNotes: Useful for adapter work but the authoritative contract remains the official CreateInvoice protocol
RuleText: Reference V-SDC integration uses the same POST /api/v3/invoices body contract together with a client certificate selected by subject CN and a PAC header while Accept-Language and RequestId remain optional headers
ExpectedError: Model transport/auth concerns separately from the fiscal invoice payload itself
Notes: Reference-only transport sample; do not elevate above the official CreateInvoice contract even though it is useful for adapter work

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
AppliesTo: Serbian codebook versus generic TaxCore buyer examples
RuleText: Generic TaxCore examples such as raw Buyer TIN strings like RS34564565 or DT12345 are not by themselves the Serbian codebook shape and Serbian buyer-identification validation must prefer the documented code-prefixed format when the Serbia buyer-identification feature is in use
TriggerContext: Reconciling jurisdiction-neutral samples with the Serbian profile
ExpectedError: Do not treat generic Buyer TIN examples as the canonical Serbian buyerId syntax
Notes: This prevents non-Serbian examples from weakening Serbian validators

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification codes 10 and 14 both denote PIB-based domestic legal-entity identifiers and should therefore reuse the same payload validator profile
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian buyer identification codes 10 or 14
ExpectedError: Do not diverge validators only because one code is the agricultural-subject branch
Notes: The business scenario differs but the identifier family is the same

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification codes 11 and 15 both denote JMBG-based domestic natural-person identifiers and should therefore reuse the same payload validator profile
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian buyer identification codes 11 or 15
ExpectedError: Do not diverge validators only because one code is the agricultural-subject branch
Notes: The business scenario differs but the identifier family is the same

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification code 12 must validate two payload segments after the prefix where the first segment follows the PIB-family validator profile and the second segment follows a numeric JBKJS-family validator profile
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian buyer identification code 12
ExpectedError: Validators should reject missing segments extra separators or non-numeric JBKJS payloads before deeper business checks
Notes: Official materials provide the composite structure and examples while stopping short of publishing an exact JBKJS digit count

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification code 16 denotes the unique number of an agricultural holding and should be validated as a number-family identifier rather than free text
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian buyer identification code 16
ExpectedError: Validators should keep code 16 aligned with numeric identifier handling unless stronger official formatting appears
Notes: The codebook names it as a unique number but does not publish the exact national digit count

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Serbian buyer identification codes 13 and 20 through 23 and 30 through 36 represent pension-card passport personal-card refugee diplomatic or residency document numbers and the reviewed official sources do not publish a stricter regex for them
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian document-number buyer identification
ExpectedError: Validators should preserve the entered document identifier subject to the code:value structure and the generic length limit instead of imposing PIB JMBG or BPG-style numeric patterns
Notes: This is an explicit choice to avoid false negatives from undocumented country-specific document formats

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Serbian buyer optional field codes 20 and 21 are form-number families while codes 30 through 33 are Tax Authority confirmation-number families and code 50 is a corporate-card number family
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian optional buyer field
ExpectedError: Validators should treat these codes as structured document-number annotations rather than generic free-text reasons
Notes: No stricter official regex is published for these subfamilies beyond the generic alphanumeric guidance

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Official-use purchases by embassies and international organizations align the buyer identification with the PIB-family branch and optional field code 20 for the SNPDV form number
TriggerContext: Special Serbian diplomatic official-use sale flow
ExpectedError: Prefer buyerId code 10 plus optional field code 20 when implementing the documented SNPDV scenario
Notes: The guidance states PIB plus SNPDV while the codebook supplies the exact optional-field code

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Personal-use purchases by diplomatic or international-organization representatives align the buyer identification with the diplomatic-ID branch and optional field code 21 for the LNPDV form number
TriggerContext: Special Serbian diplomatic personal-use sale flow
ExpectedError: Prefer buyerId code 31 plus optional field code 21 when implementing the documented LNPDV scenario
Notes: The guidance states diplomatic ID plus LNPDV while the codebook supplies the exact code pairing

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
AppliesTo: Foreign TIN prefix guidance for code 40
RuleText: When Serbian buyer identification code 40 is used the payload may preserve the generic foreign-TIN formatting guidance where an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country prefix is used and the remainder may contain digits letters spaces or hyphens
TriggerContext: Invoice with Serbian buyer identification code 40
ExpectedError: Do not strip or over-normalize country prefixes and separators from a foreign TIN unless a stronger local rule requires it
Notes: This extends the generic foreign-TIN guidance into the Serbian code-40 branch

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
RuleText: Optional buyer field is not gated only by receipt type because reviewed official sources show valid use on Promet Prodaja sale scenarios and on Promet Refundacija corporate-card aggregate refund scenarios whenever the buyer-identification codebook and the concrete business flow require it
TriggerContext: Receipt generation with buyer optional field
ExpectedError: Do not hardcode buyerCostCenterId as Promet Prodaja-only or Refund-only
Notes: The ESIR manual common-mistakes wording is treated as simplified shorthand rather than the canonical gate

LayerNotes: Lower-authority noncash refund customer-trace guidance retained as guidance
RuleText: When money is returned to the buyer card or another noncash instrument guidance material says the seller should not require an ID document or JMBG and that the buyer receipt number is sufficient for refund tracing
ExpectedError: Noncash refund UX should not force document capture before the refund path can continue
Notes: Guidance-only customer-trace rule; it does not by itself publish a canonical CreateInvoice field pattern

LayerNotes: This family is useful and evidence-based but explicitly contains inference and non-publication findings rather than direct published regexes
AppliesTo: Serbian codebook is semantic not a published national-length spec
RuleText: The reviewed Serbian codebook and technical guide publish code meanings family examples and a few explicit composite formats such as 12:PIB:JBKJS and 60:ddmmgggg_ddmmgggg but they do not publish exact national digit lengths or a full regex for PIB JMBG JBKJS BPG passport and related document numbers
ExpectedError: Do not hardcode stricter per-code national lengths unless a stronger official source is added to the corpus
Notes: This turns the remaining buyer-validator gap into an explicit non-publication finding rather than an extraction omission

LayerNotes: Lower-authority on-site refund document-choice guidance retained as guidance
AppliesTo: On-site refund guidance excludes JMBG capture
RuleText: Guidance for refunding money to the customer on site explicitly says the system should capture an ID-card number or another codebook document and not JMBG
ExpectedError: Prefer refund buyer document-number codes such as domestic ID-card code 20 over JMBG-family codes unless a stronger source defines an allowed exception
Notes: Guidance-level rule for document choice in the on-site refund branch and not a general refund-schema substitute

LayerNotes: Non-publication finding retained as derived layer after REC-RUL-011 split
AppliesTo: Noncash refund invoice shape is not published in the reviewed corpus
RuleText: The reviewed official material confirms the operational rule for noncash refunds but does not publish a dedicated CreateInvoice payload example or an explicit statement whether buyerId should be omitted preserved or replaced by another field in that branch
TriggerContext: Canonical modeling of noncash refund request shape
ExpectedError: Keep the catalog at the guidance level and avoid inventing a noncash refund field pattern that the reviewed corpus does not state
Notes: Second-pass non-publication finding; do not synthesize buyerId or buyerCostCenterId behavior that the reviewed corpus does not actually publish

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: RequestId is an optional POS-generated CreateInvoice header with max length 32 used for later request search and recovery
ExpectedError: Reject or normalize RequestId values longer than 32 before sending
Notes: Used by GetLastSignedInvoice

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: If multiple POS clients share one E-SDC the RequestId must be unique at the shared E-SDC scope and the spec recommends GUID or POS_id plus request_id
ExpectedError: Collision-free recovery lookup
Notes: Operational guidance from GetLastSignedInvoice

LayerNotes: This family mixes hard request-shape rules with manual and taxpayer-facing flow requirements
RuleText: E-SDC should store at least the latest response in non-volatile memory and keep it at least until the next request and in multi-SE setups it should index by request_id plus secure-element UID
TriggerContext: Recovery after transport failure or power loss
ExpectedError: POS can safely query the last signed invoice after failures
Notes: Operational guidance from GetLastSignedInvoice

LayerNotes: The JSON mode is explicit in the applications guidance but not part of the core LPFR protocol spec
RuleText: The public invoice verification URL supports GET with Accept application/json and returns a machine-readable verification payload without authentication
TriggerContext: Receipt verification outside ESIR or POS
ExpectedError: The same verification URL can serve human and machine clients
Notes: Applications guide documents the JSON mode explicitly

LayerNotes: These rows are helpful for future adapters but remain implementation evidence
AppliesTo: PKI applet locked state in reference reader
RuleText: Reference smart-card reader treats status word 0x6983 during PKI PIN verification as a locked PKI applet state
ExpectedError: Surface a dedicated locked state instead of a generic wrong-PIN error
Notes: Reference-only adapter UX rule and not part of the official fiscal protocol corpus

LayerNotes: These rows are helpful for future adapters but remain implementation evidence
AppliesTo: PKI remaining tries encoding in reference reader
RuleText: Reference smart-card reader interprets PKI wrong-PIN SW1 0x63 with SW2 encoded as 0xC0 plus remaining tries and the UI subtracts 192 to display the remaining attempts
ExpectedError: Expose remaining tries when available instead of discarding SW2
Notes: Reference-only adapter UX rule derived from CardReaderService and VerifyPinDialogViewModel

LayerNotes: The operational story is strongly corroborated across spec manual tests and BE rulebook materials
RuleText: VerifyPin request body must be a plain-text string containing exactly 4 ASCII digits 0 through 9
ExpectedError: Return invalid-format status instead of forwarding malformed PIN bytes
Notes: The service description makes this stricter than a generic four-character rule

LayerNotes: Public shared-service preference is explicit but remains an operational guidance rule rather than a hard transport invariant
AppliesTo: Shared TaxCore data can be sourced from SDC
RuleText: Configuration and tax-rate data are publicly available from shared TaxCore.API services but the specification explicitly says the same data can be obtained from any SDC device and that SDC retrieval is the preferred option when available
ExpectedError: Prefer local SDC bootstrap for runtime integration and use shared services as a public fallback or provisioning path
Notes: This keeps the separate contract surface visible without forcing implementations to duplicate bootstrap logic unnecessarily

LayerNotes: Backend token lifetime and reuse are explicitly documented
RuleText: Request Authentication Token returns a token that is valid for 8 hours by default and the same token must be reused for subsequent TaxCore.API E-SDC calls until expiresAt
ExpectedError: Request a new token only when the current token is missing or expired
Notes: If a new token is requested while the current one is still valid TaxCore returns the current token

LayerNotes: Backend token header requirement is explicitly documented across E-SDC services
AppliesTo: Token header required for E-SDC backend services
RuleText: All reviewed TaxCore.API E-SDC backend operations except Request Authentication Token require TaxCoreAuthenticationToken in the request headers
TriggerContext: Calling E-SDC backend services after token acquisition
ExpectedError: Do not call commands status audit or audit-proof endpoints without the token header
Notes: Applies to Get Initialization Commands Notify Online Status Notify Command Processed Submit Audit Package and Submit Audit Request Payload

LayerNotes: Notify Online Status command-return gate is explicitly documented
RuleText: Notify Online Status returns command data only when the request body indicates true while false reports the offline transition without downloading commands
ExpectedError: Do not assume a command pull occurs on offline notifications
Notes: The reviewed text alternates between quoted strings and bare JSON booleans for this body

LayerNotes: ProofOfAuditRequest composition is explicitly documented as the bridge between APDU audit data and the HTTP audit-proof endpoint
RuleText: ProofOfAuditRequest must be built from Base64 encoded Start Audit APDU bytes together with the current sum and limit returned by Amount Status
ExpectedError: Use the exact Start Audit and Amount Status outputs from the same secure-element state when creating the HTTP request
Notes: This is the bridge between the APDU audit flow and the TaxCore.API audit-proof endpoint

LayerNotes: Audit-package deletion on verified status is explicitly documented
AppliesTo: Delete audit package on verified status
RuleText: If Submit Audit Package returns audit status 4 the corresponding local audit package must be deleted immediately
ExpectedError: Verified audit packages must not stay queued in local storage
Notes: The note after AuditDataStatus marks this behavior explicitly

LayerNotes: Audit-package resubmission is explicitly restricted to status 1
AppliesTo: Retry audit package only on status 1
RuleText: E-SDC should retry Submit Audit Package only when AuditDataStatus status 1 is returned and should not retry other audit-status values
ExpectedError: Restrict automatic resubmission to the documented server-storage failure case
Notes: The note after AuditDataStatus marks status 1 as the only retryable case

LayerNotes: This captures a source-level divergence between shared Tax Rates and GetStatus currentTaxRates shapes
AppliesTo: currentTaxRates shape differs between shared and LPFR surfaces
RuleText: The reviewed corpus publishes currentTaxRates as an array in the shared Tax Rates service example but as a singular TaxRateGroup object in GetStatusResponse so serializers must not assume both surfaces have an identical shape
TriggerContext: Normalizing shared TaxCore.API and LPFR tax metadata
ExpectedError: Keep the two contracts distinct even though they share TaxRateGroup semantics
Notes: This is a source-level divergence and not just an implementation preference
